title: Card and PIN Authentication Requirement
description: The system shall require both a physical card and a valid PIN to authenticate users, ensuring secure access control.
rationale: To enhance user security by implementing a dual-factor authentication mechanism.
business_value: Improves the overall security of the system, reducing the risk of unauthorized access and protecting sensitive data.

source.description: As a user I need card and PIN authentication so that I can securely access the system.

acceptance_criteria: A registered user with a valid card and known PIN
The user inserts the card and enters their PIN
The system authenticates the user and grants access
preconditions: The user has a registered account
The user possesses a valid physical card associated with their account
The user knows their correct PIN
postconditions: Access is granted to the system for the authenticated user
No unauthorized access is granted to other users or systems
functional_constraints: The card and PIN must be inserted/entered simultaneously for authentication to proceed
The system must process the input within a specified time limit to ensure timely response
security_requirements: Two-factor authentication (card + PIN) is mandatory for user access
PINs should have a minimum length of 4 digits and maximum length of 12 digits
Card readers must be tamper-resistant and secure against unauthorized access
Authentication attempts with incorrect card or PIN combinations should result in lockout after a specified number of attempts

non_functional_requirements.performance: The system should authenticate users within 5 seconds under normal conditions
non_functional_requirements.availability: 99.9% uptime guarantee for continuous access without downtime
non_functional_requirements.scalability: The system must support up to 10,000 concurrent authentication requests per hour without performance degradation
non_functional_requirements.usability: Error messages should be clear and user-friendly; PIN entry should have visual feedback for security

edge_cases: What happens if the card is magnetic stripe instead of chip?
How does the system handle simultaneous authentication requests from multiple users?
If a user enters an incorrect PIN three times, what should the lockout period be?
What happens if the card reader malfunctions or is tampered with?

interfaces.name: Card Reader
interfaces.description: The system will communicate with a card reader to verify the physical card.

interfaces.name: PIN Verification Service
interfaces.description: The system will interact with a service that verifies the entered PIN against the user's record.

test_cases.description: User successfully enters valid card and PIN.
test_cases.expected_result: System authenticates the user and grants access.

test_cases.description: User enters an incorrect PIN three times.
test_cases.expected_result: System locks out the user after three attempts.

test_cases.description: User enters a valid card but an invalid PIN.
test_cases.expected_result: System rejects the authentication attempt and denies access.
System handles the error gracefully and prompts for retry or reports an issue to support.
